segments.text:
Listen to this, you priests! Listen, house of Israel, 
and give ear, house of the king! For the judgment is against you; for you have been a snare at Mizpah, 
and a net spread on Tabor.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The rebels are deep in slaughter, but I discipline all of them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I know Ephraim, and Israel is not hidden from me; for now, Ephraim, you have played the prostitute. Israel is defiled.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Their deeds won’t allow them to turn to their God, for the spirit of prostitution is within them, 
and they don’t know Yahweh.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
The pride of Israel testifies to his face. Therefore Israel and Ephraim will stumble in their iniquity. 
Judah also will stumble with them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
They will go with their flocks and with their herds to seek Yahweh, but they won’t find him. 
He has withdrawn himself from them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
They are unfaithful to Yahweh; for they have borne illegitimate children. 
Now the new moon will devour them with their fields.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
“Blow the cornet in Gibeah, and the trumpet in Ramah! 
Sound a battle cry at Beth Aven, behind you, Benjamin!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Ephraim will become a desolation in the day of rebuke. Among the tribes of Israel, I have made known that which will surely be.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The princes of Judah are like those who remove a landmark. I will pour out my wrath on them like water.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Ephraim is oppressed, he is crushed in judgment, 
because he is intent in his pursuit of idols.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Therefore I am to Ephraim like a moth, and to the house of Judah like rottenness.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
“When Ephraim saw his sickness, and Judah his wound, 
then Ephraim went to Assyria, 
and sent to King Jareb: but he is not able to heal you, neither will he cure you of your wound.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For I will be to Ephraim like a lion, and like a young lion to the house of Judah. I myself will tear in pieces and go away. I will carry off, and there will be no one to deliver.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
I will go and return to my place, until they acknowledge their offense, 
and seek my face. 
In their affliction they will seek me earnestly.
segments.speaker: The LORD
